vocabulary.en: a project to restore something to good condition
vocabulary.excerpt: this comes after his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed
vocabulary.example_cn: 这座历史建筑的修复工程耗资巨大。

vocabulary.en: full of something unpleasant or bad
vocabulary.excerpt: the water riddled with algae
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的文章充满了错误。

vocabulary.en: the presence of police or other authorities
vocabulary.excerpt: Visitors say they noticed an increased law enforcement presence
vocabulary.example_cn: 抗议活动期间，该地区加强了执法力量。

vocabulary.en: to pursue legal action as severely as possible
vocabulary.excerpt: These are cases that will be prosecuted to the full extent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 警方表示将依法严惩肇事者。

vocabulary.en: a contract awarded without competitive bidding
vocabulary.excerpt: this was a no-bid contract that was given to a pool company
vocabulary.example_cn: 非竞标合同往往引发腐败担忧。

vocabulary.en: holding false beliefs, especially as a result of mental illness
vocabulary.excerpt: I feel like is delusional
vocabulary.example_cn: 他认为自己能当总统，简直是妄想。

vocabulary.en: a person who paddles a canoe
vocabulary.excerpt: One of those arrested was a three-time Olympian canoeist
vocabulary.example_cn: 这位奥运独木舟选手赢得了金牌。

vocabulary.en: a prolonged bitter quarrel with or campaign against someone
vocabulary.excerpt: she's shown herself to be the person who's willing to take on Trump's sometimes more political vendettas
vocabulary.example_cn: 两个家族之间的宿怨持续了数十年。

vocabulary.cn: 唯一的观众（指只为取悦某人）
vocabulary.en: a situation where someone acts only to please one particular person
vocabulary.excerpt: they have an audience of one, and they're going to serve that audience
vocabulary.example_cn: 这位政客的言论只是为了取悦总统这个唯一的观众。

vocabulary.en: a story about magical events, often used metaphorically for an amazing success
vocabulary.excerpt: Cape Verde and their fairy tale continues
vocabulary.example_cn: 这支弱队的胜利简直是一个童话。

vocabulary.en: the stage of a tournament where losing teams are eliminated
vocabulary.excerpt: one step closer to a legitimate chance of getting into the knockout stage
vocabulary.example_cn: 球队成功晋级淘汰赛阶段。

vocabulary.en: a conflict between different cultural values or practices
vocabulary.excerpt: Why do you think all of this, like, culture clash is such a big deal?
vocabulary.example_cn: 国际交流中难免出现文化冲突。

vocabulary.en: in a way that is unfair or of poor quality
vocabulary.excerpt: The Iranian team, in particular, has been treated pretty shabbily by the U.S. government
vocabulary.example_cn: 这些工人受到了不公正的对待。

vocabulary.en: a possibility that is not zero, meaning it could happen
vocabulary.excerpt: There is a non-zero possibility Iran certainly looks like they could advance
vocabulary.example_cn: 他获胜的可能性非零。

vocabulary.en: a series of tornadoes occurring in a short period
vocabulary.excerpt: At least two people are dead after a tornado outbreak in the Mideast.
vocabulary.example_cn: 龙卷风爆发导致多人伤亡。

vocabulary.en: recorded officially
vocabulary.excerpt: More than three dozen tornado reports were logged in the region by the storm prediction center.
vocabulary.example_cn: 风暴预测中心记录了该地区超过36份龙卷风报告。

vocabulary.en: a method that causes disagreement
vocabulary.excerpt: It was a controversial tactic aimed at building larger criminal cases against drug traffickers.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一种有争议的策略，旨在对毒贩提起更大的刑事诉讼。

vocabulary.en: very long and intense negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: Sunday the two sides held marathon talks to try to establish a long term peace agreement with Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: 周日双方举行了马拉松式谈判，试图与伊朗达成长期和平协议。

vocabulary.en: to give up a job or position
vocabulary.excerpt: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, resigning.
vocabulary.example_cn: 英国首相基尔·斯塔默辞职。

vocabulary.en: in a willing and pleasant manner
vocabulary.excerpt: And I accept that answer with good grace.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我欣然接受这个答案。

vocabulary.en: the period during which someone holds a position
vocabulary.excerpt: Starmer's resignation comes just shy of two years into his tenure.
vocabulary.example_cn: 斯塔默辞职时任期刚满两年。

vocabulary.en: frequent change
vocabulary.excerpt: there's been almost half a dozen prime ministers since Brexit, and it's been a decade since Brexit. So, I think, to the American public, we see a lot of churn.
vocabulary.example_cn: 自英国脱欧以来，已有近六位首相，美国公众看到很多变动。

vocabulary.en: an official order or commission to do something
vocabulary.excerpt: when Starmer got elected two years ago on this mandate of change
vocabulary.example_cn: 两年前斯塔默以变革的授权当选。

vocabulary.en: mistakes made without pressure from opponents
vocabulary.excerpt: they basically made a series of what felt like unforced errors right at the start
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们一开始就犯了一系列非受迫性失误。

vocabulary.en: a payment to help elderly people with heating costs
vocabulary.excerpt: They decided to cut winter fuel allowance for elderly people
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们决定削减老年人的冬季燃料补贴。

vocabulary.en: an action or event causing public outrage
vocabulary.excerpt: there was a scandal over Keir Starmer accepting free gifts as prime minister
vocabulary.example_cn: 斯塔默接受免费礼物引发丑闻。

vocabulary.en: the way something is presented or perceived
vocabulary.excerpt: set the framing, set the tone for people to kind of start to wonder
vocabulary.example_cn: 设定了框架和基调，让人们开始怀疑。

vocabulary.en: the ability to make considered decisions
vocabulary.excerpt: his judgment has really been called into question over that
vocabulary.example_cn: 他的判断力因此受到质疑。

vocabulary.en: a very exciting or turbulent experience
vocabulary.excerpt: it's been a wild ride
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是一段疯狂的旅程。

vocabulary.en: to play music loudly
vocabulary.excerpt: protesters who were blasting the anthem for the E.U.
vocabulary.example_cn: 抗议者大声播放欧盟国歌。

vocabulary.en: to make less severe
vocabulary.excerpt: an immigration surge as U.K. tried to mitigate some of the concerns of Brexit
vocabulary.example_cn: 英国试图减轻脱欧担忧，导致移民激增。

vocabulary.en: rising in active revolt
vocabulary.excerpt: reforms pitch is very much, we are the insurgent populist party
vocabulary.example_cn: 改革党的定位是：我们是反叛的民粹主义政党。

vocabulary.en: to destroy completely
vocabulary.excerpt: can actually smash up the status quo
vocabulary.example_cn: 能够粉碎现状。

vocabulary.en: a clever escape from a difficult situation
vocabulary.excerpt: it's kind of a bit of a Houdini act
vocabulary.example_cn: 这有点像逃脱术。

vocabulary.en: to strengthen one's commitment to a course of action
vocabulary.excerpt: the president has doubled down on his feud with the prime minister there
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统加倍坚持与意大利总理的争执。

vocabulary.en: a prolonged and bitter quarrel
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump's Feud with Italian PM
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普与意大利总理的不和。

vocabulary.en: asking for something in a desperate way
vocabulary.excerpt: accused the Italian prime minister of begging for a photo with him
vocabulary.example_cn: 指责意大利总理乞求与他合影。

vocabulary.en: invented; not true
vocabulary.excerpt: Donald Trump's statements are completely made up.
vocabulary.example_cn: 唐纳德·特朗普的言论完全是编造的。

vocabulary.en: a loyal and reliable ally
vocabulary.excerpt: Giorgia Meloni is somebody who is seen as a staunch ally of the president.
vocabulary.example_cn: 乔治娅·梅洛尼被视为总统的坚定盟友。

vocabulary.en: a petty quarrel
vocabulary.excerpt: this spat has continued
vocabulary.example_cn: 这场口角持续不断。

vocabulary.en: the feeling of being upset or annoyed
vocabulary.excerpt: his larger frustration with NATO allies
vocabulary.example_cn: 他对北约盟友的更大挫折感。

vocabulary.en: to make someone feel ashamed
vocabulary.excerpt: humiliates Meloni
vocabulary.example_cn: 羞辱梅洛尼。

vocabulary.en: to announce in advance
vocabulary.excerpt: He kicks dirt over it by pre-announcing it.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他通过提前宣布来搅局。

vocabulary.en: to take a risk
vocabulary.excerpt: Newsom is reportedly gambling on embracing former President Joe Biden
vocabulary.example_cn: 据报道，纽森冒险拥抱前总统乔·拜登。

vocabulary.en: to restore to a good condition or reputation
vocabulary.excerpt: trying to rehabilitate
vocabulary.example_cn: 试图恢复声誉。

vocabulary.en: posting provocative messages to elicit reactions
vocabulary.excerpt: He's trolling, yes.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他在挑衅。

vocabulary.en: to navigate a difficult situation skillfully
vocabulary.excerpt: I think he's trying to thread this needle
vocabulary.example_cn: 我认为他试图巧妙应对。

sentences.en: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election.
sentences.cn: 我的政党现在提出的问题是，我是否是最适合带领我们进入下一届大选的人。
sentences.structure: 主句：The question...is whether...；my party is asking now 是定语从句修饰 question；whether 引导表语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主语是 The question，后面跟了一个定语从句 my party is asking now，系动词 is 后接 whether 引导的表语从句。表语从句中，I 是主语，am best placed 是谓语，to lead us into the next general election 是不定式短语作目的状语。

sentences.en: Every decision I've taken has been about putting the country I love first.
sentences.cn: 我所做的每一个决定都是为了把我热爱的国家放在第一位。
sentences.structure: 主句：Every decision...has been about putting...；I've taken 是定语从句修饰 decision；I love 是定语从句修饰 country。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主语 Every decision 后跟定语从句 I've taken，谓语 has been about，介词 about 后接动名词短语 putting the country I love first，其中 country 后又有定语从句 I love。

sentences.en: But the real clinching factor was that we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May, and the Labour Party suffered a massive defeat, losing more than 1,400 local council seats, even as we saw the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge.
sentences.cn: 但真正的决定性因素是，我们在5月份英国各地举行了地方选举，工党遭遇了重大失败，失去了1400多个地方议会席位，与此同时，我们看到由奈杰尔·法拉奇领导的右翼民粹主义改革党崛起。
sentences.structure: 主句：the real clinching factor was that...；that 引导表语从句，从句由 and 连接两个并列分句；第一个分句：we had local elections...；第二个分句：the Labour Party suffered...；losing... 是现在分词短语作结果状语；even as 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句主语是 the real clinching factor，系动词 was，后接 that 引导的表语从句。表语从句包含两个并列分句，由 and 连接。第一个分句是 we had local elections...，第二个分句是 the Labour Party suffered a massive defeat，后面 losing... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，even as 引导时间状语从句，其中 led by Nigel Farage 是过去分词短语作定语修饰 Reform Party。

sentences.en: So, then this process started to try and get him back into Parliament.
sentences.cn: 于是，这个过程开始了，试图让他重返议会。
sentences.structure: 主句：this process started to try and get him back into Parliament。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，主语 this process，谓语 started，to try and get him back into Parliament 是不定式短语作宾语，其中 try and get 是口语化表达，相当于 try to get。

sentences.en: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week, which he won so convincingly that it really strengthened the argument that he might be the man to defeat this right-wing populist party, Reform, in any upcoming election, since he had done it so decisively in this one constituency.
sentences.cn: 上周在英格兰西北部举行了一场特别选举，他以令人信服的方式获胜，这确实强化了一种论点，即他可能是任何即将到来的选举中击败右翼民粹主义政党改革党的人，因为他在这个选区如此果断地做到了这一点。
sentences.structure: 主句：There was a special election...；that 引导定语从句修饰 election；which 引导非限制性定语从句修饰整个主句；so...that... 结构；that 引导同位语从句修饰 argument；since 引导原因状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是 There was a special election，后跟 that 引导的定语从句修饰 election。which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代整个主句，从句中包含 so...that... 结构，that 引导结果状语从句。结果状语从句中，argument 后跟 that 引导的同位语从句，说明 argument 的内容。最后 since 引导原因状语从句。

sentences.en: I will remain in post as prime minister until the contest is complete, and I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power.
sentences.cn: 我将继续担任首相，直到竞选结束，我将尽我所能确保权力的有序移交。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由 and 连接。第一个分句：I will remain...until...；第二个分句：I will do everything...；I can 是定语从句修饰 everything。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列复合句。第一个分句包含 until 引导的时间状语从句。第二个分句中，everything 后跟定语从句 I can，省略了关系代词 that，to ensure... 是不定式短语作目的状语。

sentences.en: I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support, knowing that they will inherit a Britain that is far stronger and fairer than the one I inherited two years ago; better prepared for the challenges ahead; and better able to ensure the Labour Party secures a second term in office.
sentences.cn: 我还将给予我的继任者充分而明确的支持，因为我知道他们将继承一个比我两年前继承的更强大、更公平的英国；更好地为未来的挑战做好准备；并且更有能力确保工党赢得第二个任期。
sentences.structure: 主句：I will also give...；knowing that... 是现在分词短语作原因状语；that 引导宾语从句；从句中 a Britain 后跟 that 引导的定语从句；than the one 是比较结构；I inherited 是定语从句修饰 the one；分号连接三个并列的形容词短语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句是 I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support，现在分词短语 knowing that... 作原因状语。that 引导的宾语从句中，主语 they，谓语 will inherit，宾语 a Britain，后跟 that 引导的定语从句。定语从句中包含比较结构 than the one，the one 后跟定语从句 I inherited。分号后是三个并列的形容词短语，修饰 Britain。

sentences.en: And pointing back to those local elections at the beginning of May, where Reform did so well.
sentences.cn: 并回顾了5月初的那些地方选举，改革党在这些选举中表现非常好。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语 pointing back to... 作状语；where 引导定语从句修饰 elections。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个现在分词短语作状语的结构，pointing back to 的逻辑主语是前文提到的 Farage。where 引导定语从句修饰 local elections。

sentences.en: But as of now, in, you know, opinion polls based on voter intentions for right now, they are leading in the country.
sentences.cn: 但截至目前，在基于当前选民意向的民意调查中，他们在全国领先。
sentences.structure: 主句：they are leading...；based on... 是过去分词短语作定语修饰 polls。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，主语 they，谓语 are leading，in the country 是地点状语。based on voter intentions for right now 是过去分词短语作定语修饰 opinion polls。

sentences.en: And the chance now for Labour under a new leader is to try to stabilize their grip on power so they can see off Reform in a general election when it happens.
sentences.cn: 现在工党在新领导人的领导下，有机会试图稳定他们对权力的掌控，以便在大选到来时能够击败改革党。
sentences.structure: 主句：the chance...is to try...；so 引导目的状语从句；when 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句主语 the chance，for Labour under a new leader 是介词短语作定语，系动词 is，不定式短语 to try to stabilize... 作表语。so 引导目的状语从句，从句中又包含 when 引导的时间状语从句。

sentences.en: President Trump says he knows what is causing the problems at the Reflecting Pool.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统说他知道了是什么导致了倒影池的问题。
sentences.structure: 主句：President Trump says...；后跟宾语从句 he knows...；what 引导名词性从句作 knows 的宾语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句主语 President Trump，谓语 says，后接宾语从句 he knows what is causing the problems at the Reflecting Pool。宾语从句中，he 是主语，knows 是谓语，what is causing the problems at the Reflecting Pool 是 what 引导的名词性从句作 knows 的宾语。

sentences.en: The president now claiming that multiple people have been arrested, accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
sentences.cn: 总统现在声称，多人已被逮捕，被指控破坏林肯纪念堂倒影池。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语 claiming that... 作伴随状语；that 引导宾语从句；accused of... 是过去分词短语作定语修饰 people。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个现在分词短语作伴随状语的结构，逻辑主语是 the president。claiming 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，从句中 accused of... 是过去分词短语作定语修饰 people。

sentences.en: Now, this comes after his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed: the water riddled with algae, the lining ripped.
sentences.cn: 现在，这发生在他耗资1400万美元的修复工程失败之后：水中充满藻类，衬里破裂。
sentences.structure: 主句：this comes after...；after 引导时间状语从句；冒号后是两个独立主格结构。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句 this comes，after 引导时间状语从句。冒号后是两个独立主格结构：the water riddled with algae 和 the lining ripped，补充说明失败的具体情况。

sentences.en: They came up to me and two other women, asking if we touched it and then promoting us not to, that we would get arrested or charged with vandalism if we did.
sentences.cn: 他们走到我和另外两名妇女面前，问我们是否碰了它，然后劝我们不要碰，说如果我们碰了就会被逮捕或被指控破坏公物。
sentences.structure: 主句：They came up to me and two other women；asking... 和 promoting... 是现在分词短语作伴随状语；if 引导宾语从句；that 引导宾语从句（promoting 的宾语）；if 引导条件状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句主语 They，谓语 came up to。两个现在分词短语 asking... 和 promoting... 作伴随状语。asking 后跟 if 引导的宾语从句。promoting 后跟 that 引导的宾语从句，其中包含 if 引导的条件状语从句。

sentences.en: If there are more serious products that are put into the Reflecting Pool to create more algae or a bigger problem, then we'll consider more serious charges.
sentences.cn: 如果有更严重的物质被倒入倒影池，导致更多藻类或更严重的问题，那么我们将考虑更严重的指控。
sentences.structure: If 引导条件状语从句；从句中 that 引导定语从句修饰 products；主句：we'll consider...。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。If 引导条件状语从句，从句中 that 引导定语从句修饰 products，to create... 是不定式短语作目的状语。主句是 we'll consider more serious charges。

sentences.en: It's interesting that this came after we saw a video of hydrogen peroxide being poured into the Reflecting Pool.
sentences.cn: 有趣的是，这发生在我们看到一段将过氧化氢倒入倒影池的视频之后。
sentences.structure: It 作形式主语，that 引导主语从句；从句中 after 引导时间状语从句；being poured 是现在分词短语作定语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个主语从句结构。It 是形式主语，真正的主语是 that 引导的从句。从句中，after 引导时间状语从句，其中 of hydrogen peroxide being poured into the Reflecting Pool 是介词短语作定语修饰 video，being poured 是现在分词短语的被动形式。

sentences.en: And it was also supposed to be done within two weeks.
sentences.cn: 而且它本应在两周内完成。
sentences.structure: 主句：it was also supposed to be done...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，主语 it，谓语 was supposed，不定式短语 to be done within two weeks 作宾语。

sentences.en: But look, we know what's at the heart of this, which is that Donald Trump cares a lot about the way that Washington, D.C., looks in this moment, particularly as it comes to the 250th anniversary just a couple of weeks away.
sentences.cn: 但你看，我们知道这背后的核心是什么，那就是唐纳德·特朗普非常关心华盛顿特区此刻的样子，尤其是距离250周年纪念日只有几周了。
sentences.structure: 主句：we know...；what 引导名词性从句作 know 的宾语；which 引导非限制性定语从句修饰 what 从句；that 引导表语从句；the way 后跟 that 引导的定语从句；as 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句 we know，后接 what 引导的名词性从句作宾语。which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代整个 what 从句。定语从句中，that 引导表语从句，表语从句中 the way 后跟 that 引导的定语从句，as 引导时间状语从句。

sentences.en: And one can argue that there are some fountains that are now running in places like Malcolm X Park or outside Union Station, which it is nice to have fountains.
sentences.cn: 有人可能会说，有些喷泉现在在马尔科姆·X公园或联合车站外等地方运行，有喷泉是件好事。
sentences.structure: 主句：one can argue that...；that 引导宾语从句；从句中 that 引导定语从句修饰 fountains；which 引导非限制性定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句 one can argue，后接 that 引导的宾语从句。宾语从句中，there are some fountains 是 there be 句型，后跟 that 引导的定语从句修饰 fountains。which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代前面整个情况。

sentences.en: And telling people that this public property, which belongs to us, the American people, we can't touch it now, because we are the ones who have vandalized it, I feel like is delusional.
sentences.cn: 告诉人们这个属于我们美国人民的公共财产，我们现在不能碰它，因为我们是破坏它的人，我觉得这简直是妄想。
sentences.structure: 主句：I feel like is delusional；telling people that... 是动名词短语作主语；that 引导宾语从句；which 引导非限制性定语从句修饰 property；because 引导原因状语从句；who 引导定语从句修饰 ones。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。动名词短语 telling people that... 作主语，谓语是 is delusional，I feel like 是插入语。telling 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，从句中 which 引导非限制性定语从句修饰 property，because 引导原因状语从句，其中 who 引导定语从句修饰 ones。

sentences.en: One of those arrested was a three-time Olympian canoeist who actually gave interviews afterwards, saying that he just bent down.
sentences.cn: 被捕者中有一位是三次参加奥运会的独木舟选手，他事后接受了采访，说他只是弯下腰。
sentences.structure: 主句：One of those arrested was a three-time Olympian canoeist；who 引导定语从句；saying that... 是现在分词短语作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句主语 One of those arrested，系动词 was，表语 a three-time Olympian canoeist。who 引导定语从句修饰 canoeist。saying that... 是现在分词短语作伴随状语，其中 that 引导宾语从句。

sentences.en: And I think it says a lot that Jeanine Pirro is the one leading the charge because she's shown herself, again and again, to be the person who's willing to take on Trump's sometimes more political, not to say vendettas, but certainly, agendas against people.
sentences.cn: 我认为这在很大程度上说明，珍妮·皮罗是带头的人，因为她一次又一次地证明自己是愿意承担特朗普有时更具政治性、甚至可以说是仇杀、但肯定是针对他人的议程的人。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think...；后跟宾语从句 it says a lot that...；that 引导主语从句；because 引导原因状语从句；who 引导定语从句修饰 person。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句 I think，后接宾语从句 it says a lot that...。宾语从句中，it 是形式主语，真正的主语是 that 引导的从句。because 引导原因状语从句，从句中 who 引导定语从句修饰 person。

sentences.en: She was the -- obviously, same prosecutor who led the investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the renovation of the Fed's headquarters.
sentences.cn: 她显然就是那位领导调查美联储主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔关于美联储总部翻新事件的检察官。
sentences.structure: 主句：She was the same prosecutor；who 引导定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句主语 She，系动词 was，表语 the same prosecutor。who 引导定语从句修饰 prosecutor，从句中 over the renovation... 是介词短语作状语。

sentences.en: So, we're bringing to bear the power of the state, the legal law enforcement arm of the district to solve a public relations problem.
sentences.cn: 所以，我们正在动用国家的力量，即该地区的法律执法机构，来解决一个公共关系问题。
sentences.structure: 主句：we're bringing to bear the power...；the legal law enforcement arm... 是同位语；to solve... 是不定式短语作目的状语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句。主语 we，谓语 are bringing to bear，宾语 the power of the state。the legal law enforcement arm of the district 是同位语，解释 the power of the state。to solve a public relations problem 是不定式短语作目的状语。

sentences.en: But Jeanine Pirro, like many people in Trump's cabinet, understands that they have an audience of one, and they're going to serve that audience.
sentences.cn: 但珍妮·皮罗和特朗普内阁中的许多人一样，明白他们只有一个观众，他们将服务于那个观众。
sentences.structure: 主句：Jeanine Pirro understands that...；like many people... 是插入语；that 引导宾语从句，从句由 and 连接两个并列分句。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句主语 Jeanine Pirro，谓语 understands，后接 that 引导的宾语从句。like many people in Trump's cabinet 是插入语。宾语从句包含两个并列分句，由 and 连接。

sentences.en: Many people probably wrote us off or didn't know much about us, and that was sort of our motivation, as well.
sentences.cn: 很多人可能认为我们不行，或者对我们了解不多，这也算是我们的动力。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由 and 连接。第一个分句：Many people wrote us off or didn't know...；第二个分句：that was sort of our motivation。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列句。第一个分句主语 Many people，谓语 wrote off 和 didn't know，宾语 us 和 about us。第二个分句主语 that，系动词 was，表语 sort of our motivation。

sentences.en: And the worries about filling stadiums and selling tickets at this point are gone because of moments like these.
sentences.cn: 而关于填满体育场和售票的担忧，因为这样的时刻已经消失了。
sentences.structure: 主句：the worries...are gone；because of 是介词短语作原因状语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句。主语 the worries，后跟介词短语 about filling stadiums and selling tickets 作定语，谓语 are gone，because of moments like these 是介词短语作原因状语。

sentences.en: Whether we're watching the next greats score their first World Cup goal or seeing a team claim a World Cup victory for the first time in their history, perhaps one of the most uniting things: seeing different cultures embracing.
sentences.cn: 无论我们是在观看下一位伟大球员打入他们的第一个世界杯进球，还是看到一支球队历史上首次赢得世界杯胜利，也许最团结的事情之一就是看到不同文化的拥抱。
sentences.structure: Whether...or... 引导让步状语从句；主句：perhaps one of the most uniting things: seeing...
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。Whether...or... 引导让步状语从句，从句中 we're watching... 和 seeing... 是并列结构。主句是 perhaps one of the most uniting things: seeing different cultures embracing，冒号后的内容是对 one of the most uniting things 的解释。

sentences.en: I don't necessarily know if the United States was the best place for the World Cup, but I do feel like it is a very important place for this to be happening because of the amount of healing that is probably happening to our society.
sentences.cn: 我不一定知道美国是否是世界杯的最佳举办地，但我确实觉得这是一个非常重要的事件发生地，因为我们的社会可能正在发生大量的疗愈。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由 but 连接。第一个分句：I don't know if...；if 引导宾语从句。第二个分句：I do feel like...；like 引导宾语从句；because of 是介词短语作原因状语；that 引导定语从句修饰 healing。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个并列复合句。第一个分句包含 if 引导的宾语从句。第二个分句包含 like 引导的宾语从句，从句中 because of 是介词短语作原因状语，其中 that 引导定语从句修饰 healing。

sentences.en: And I feel like, for all the worries that like, listen, ICE has not been a major presence in this tournament, like a lot of people worried about.
sentences.cn: 我觉得，尽管有种种担忧，比如，听着，移民和海关执法局并没有像很多人担心的那样成为本届赛事的主要存在。
sentences.structure: 主句：I feel like...；for all the worries 是介词短语作让步状语；that 引导定语从句修饰 worries；like a lot of people worried about 是插入语。
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句。主句 I feel like，后接宾语从句（省略了 that）。for all the worries 是介词短语作让步状语，that 引导定语从句修饰 worries。like a lot of people worried about 是插入语，表示举例。

sentences.en: At least two people are dead after a tornado outbreak in the Mideast. More -- in the Midwest. More than three dozen tornado reports were logged in the region by the storm prediction center.
sentences.cn: 中西部龙卷风爆发后至少两人死亡。风暴预测中心记录了该地区超过36份龙卷风报告。
sentences.structure: 简单句+并列句
sentences.analysis: 第一句为简单句，主语two people，系动词are，表语dead，后接时间状语after...。第二句为并列句，主语More than three dozen tornado reports，谓语were logged，状语by...。

sentences.en: It was a controversial tactic aimed at building larger criminal cases against drug traffickers.
sentences.cn: 这是一种有争议的策略，旨在对毒贩提起更大的刑事诉讼。
sentences.structure: 简单句+过去分词短语作定语
sentences.analysis: 主句It was a controversial tactic，过去分词短语aimed at...作定语修饰tactic。

sentences.en: Sunday the two sides held marathon talks to try to establish a long term peace agreement with Iran.
sentences.cn: 周日双方举行了马拉松式谈判，试图与伊朗达成长期和平协议。
sentences.structure: 简单句+不定式短语作目的状语
sentences.analysis: 主语the two sides，谓语held，宾语marathon talks，不定式短语to try...作目的状语。

sentences.en: Starmer's resignation comes just shy of two years into his tenure.
sentences.cn: 斯塔默辞职时任期刚满两年。
sentences.structure: 简单句
sentences.analysis: 主语Starmer's resignation，谓语comes，状语just shy of two years into his tenure。

sentences.en: I think when Starmer got elected two years ago on this mandate of change, that was the Labour Party's slogan into the last election almost exactly two years ago, there was a real desire and a real hope that they would be different to the conservative party that had gone before, who had, as you say, had run through a whole host of different prime ministers.
sentences.cn: 我认为，当斯塔默两年前以变革的授权当选时——这几乎是两年前大选中工党的口号——人们确实渴望并希望他们会不同于之前的保守党，后者正如你所说，经历了一连串不同的首相。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含宾语从句、定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接宾语从句when...。宾语从句中嵌套定语从句that they would be different...，其中又包含定语从句that had gone before和who had run through...。

sentences.en: They decided to cut winter fuel allowance for elderly people in a move that was kind of seen as really typically un-Labour.
sentences.cn: 他们决定削减老年人的冬季燃料补贴，这一举动被视为非常不符合工党传统。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句They decided to cut...，后接定语从句that was kind of seen as...修饰a move。

sentences.en: And also there was a scandal over Keir Starmer accepting free gifts as prime minister, like a suit, glasses, those kind of things.
sentences.cn: 还有一起关于基尔·斯塔默作为首相接受免费礼物的丑闻，比如西装、眼镜之类的东西。
sentences.structure: 简单句+动名词复合结构
sentences.analysis: 主句there was a scandal，后接over Keir Starmer accepting...，其中accepting为动名词，Keir Starmer为其逻辑主语。

sentences.en: And I think that is really what's thrown it more recently into a kind of state of crisis.
sentences.cn: 我认为这正是最近使其陷入危机状态的原因。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接宾语从句that is...，其中what's thrown...为表语从句。

sentences.en: Essentially, what we've seen over the past decade is British voters trying to, in various ways, push a button marked change, whether that was for Brexit, whether that was for Boris Johnson, who was saying, I'm going to get Brexit done.
sentences.cn: 本质上，我们在过去十年看到的是英国选民试图以各种方式按下“变革”按钮，无论是为了脱欧，还是为了鲍里斯·约翰逊——他曾说“我要完成脱欧”。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含主语从句、定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语从句what we've seen...，系动词is，表语British voters trying...，后接whether...whether...并列状语从句，其中包含定语从句who was saying...。

sentences.en: And so, reforms pitch is very much, we are the insurgent populist party that can actually smash up the status quo, smash up the establishment and finally deliver change.
sentences.cn: 因此，改革党的定位是：我们是反叛的民粹主义政党，能够粉碎现状、粉碎建制派，最终带来变革。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句reforms pitch is very much，后接直接引语we are...，其中包含定语从句that can actually smash up...。

sentences.en: But it's kind of a bit of a Houdini act, really, because, obviously, Nigel Farage was one of the main architects of Brexit in the first place and many feel that Brexit hasn't been a success.
sentences.cn: 但这有点像逃脱术，因为显然奈杰尔·法拉奇最初就是脱欧的主要设计者之一，而许多人认为脱欧并不成功。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含原因状语从句和宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句it's kind of...，后接because引导的原因状语从句，其中包含宾语从句that Brexit hasn't been a success。

sentences.en: And I think it is really notable that this spat has continued because I think it reflects his larger frustration with NATO allies over this U.S.-Iran conflict.
sentences.cn: 我认为值得注意的是，这场口角持续不断，因为我认为这反映了他对北约盟友在美伊冲突问题上的更大挫折感。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含主语从句和原因状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接宾语从句it is really notable that...，其中that引导主语从句，后接because引导的原因状语从句。

sentences.en: And let's not forget that this spat sort of started a couple of weeks ago when he -- when Donald Trump attacked the pope and then Giorgia Meloni, you know, defended the pope in light of the U.S.-Iran War.
sentences.cn: 别忘了，这场口角大约几周前就开始了，当时唐纳德·特朗普攻击教皇，然后乔治娅·梅洛尼因美伊战争而捍卫教皇。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含宾语从句和定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句let's not forget，后接宾语从句that this spat...，其中包含when引导的定语从句修饰a couple of weeks ago。

sentences.en: But, you know, taking a step back from that, it's fascinating to see sort of, you know, I guess, I don't want to say the decline, but maybe the tensions that are rising between Trump and many European leaders, like Meloni, at this time during NATO -- because of the Iraq war, NATO tensions, et cetera.
sentences.cn: 但退一步看，看到特朗普与许多欧洲领导人（如梅洛尼）之间紧张关系上升是很有趣的，尤其是在北约期间——由于伊拉克战争、北约紧张局势等原因。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句it's fascinating to see...，后接宾语the tensions，定语从句that are rising...修饰tensions。

sentences.en: And we've seen similar splits with folks who, again, think similarly to him in France and Germany.
sentences.cn: 我们在法国和德国那些与他想法相似的人中也看到了类似的分裂。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句we've seen similar splits，后接定语从句who think similarly to him修饰folks。

sentences.en: It's not just Carney in Canada who is not on the same page as Trump on many of these things to begin with.
sentences.cn: 不仅仅是加拿大的卡尼一开始就在许多事情上与特朗普意见不合。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句It's not just Carney，后接定语从句who is not on the same page...。

sentences.en: And because -- it's a political calculation.
sentences.cn: 因为——这是一个政治算计。
sentences.structure: 简单句
sentences.analysis: 省略句，完整为And it's a political calculation because...。

sentences.en: They know that Trump is not only polling well in the United States, but he's not polling well in their countries.
sentences.cn: 他们知道特朗普不仅在美国民调不佳，在他们国家民调也不好。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句They know，后接宾语从句that Trump is not only...but...。

sentences.en: And so, if you've -- you're looking to pull yourself up a little bit, this is maybe an excuse to do it by leaning into these clashes that Trump seems so willing to start unprompted.
sentences.cn: 因此，如果你想稍微提升自己，这也许是一个借口，通过利用特朗普似乎乐于主动挑起的冲突来实现。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含条件状语从句和定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句this is maybe an excuse，后接条件状语从句if you're looking...，定语从句that Trump seems...修饰clashes。

sentences.en: Let's not forget where a lot of this also started, which was his attempt to take over Greenland, where he refused to rule out military force in an attempt to go after a NATO ally.
sentences.cn: 别忘了，其中很多也始于他试图接管格陵兰岛，当时他拒绝排除使用武力来对付一个北约盟友。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含宾语从句和定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句Let's not forget，后接宾语从句where a lot of this also started，非限制性定语从句which was his attempt...，其中又包含定语从句where he refused...。

sentences.en: This is reportedly the most flooding the area has seen in decades.
sentences.cn: 据报道，这是该地区几十年来最严重的洪水。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句This is reportedly the most flooding，后接定语从句the area has seen in decades。

sentences.en: Newsom is reportedly gambling on embracing former President Joe Biden, thinking that will be a popular position among Democrats.
sentences.cn: 据报道，纽森冒险拥抱前总统乔·拜登，认为这将在民主党中受欢迎。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句Newsom is reportedly gambling on embracing...，现在分词thinking引导伴随状语，后接宾语从句that will be...。

sentences.en: Even after Biden had to drop out of his re-election bid, Newsom has still spoken favorably of Biden's presidency.
sentences.cn: 即使在拜登不得不退出连任竞选后，纽森仍然对拜登的总统任期给予好评。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含时间状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句Newsom has still spoken favorably...，时间状语从句Even after Biden had to drop out...。

sentences.en: And we'll continue to build on the legacy, I would argue, of our former president, who I think was one of the most successful presidents in the last century, and that is Joe Biden.
sentences.cn: 而且我们将继续在前总统的遗产基础上发展，我认为他是上世纪最成功的总统之一，那就是乔·拜登。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句和插入语
sentences.analysis: 主句we'll continue to build on the legacy，插入语I would argue，定语从句who I think was...修饰former president。

sentences.en: Very difficult calculus to do that I think a lot of Democratic hopefuls have decided is not possible.
sentences.cn: 我认为许多民主党希望者认为这是不可能的，这是一个非常困难的算计。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句Very difficult calculus，后接定语从句that I think a lot of Democratic hopefuls have decided is not possible。

sentences.en: But I don't think anyone can accuse Gavin Newsom of not being a very calculating politician.
sentences.cn: 但我认为没有人能指责加文·纽森不是一个非常精于算计的政治家。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I don't think，后接宾语从句anyone can accuse...。

sentences.en: And I think that there is a -- an awareness that you can't just alienate those folks by saying, let's throw out Joe Biden altogether.
sentences.cn: 我认为有一种意识，你不能仅仅通过说“让我们彻底抛弃乔·拜登”来疏远那些人。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含同位语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接宾语从句that there is an awareness，同位语从句that you can't...解释awareness。

sentences.en: I think he's trying to thread this needle of acknowledging the successes that Joe Biden had.
sentences.cn: 我认为他试图巧妙应对，承认乔·拜登取得的成功。
sentences.structure: 复合句，包含定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句I think，后接宾语从句he's trying to thread this needle，定语从句that Joe Biden had修饰successes。

sentences.en: And I think what's going to come out of those races, those primaries, is going to be so telling about where the Democratic Party is headed.
sentences.cn: 我认为这些竞选和初选的结果将非常能说明民主党的走向。

sentences.en: That's always the thing.
sentences.cn: 事情总是这样。

topics.title: 英国政治：基尔·斯塔默辞职
topics.content: 英国首相基尔·斯塔默在面临党内压力后宣布辞职。工党在地方选举中惨败，失去1400多个席位，而右翼民粹主义改革党崛起。曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆被视为潜在继任者，他在补选中获胜，增强了接班的呼声。斯塔默将留任至新领袖选出。
topics.keywords: Keir Starmer, resignation, Labour Party, Andy Burnham, Reform Party, Nigel Farage, local elections

topics.title: 美国政治：倒影池争议
topics.content: 特朗普总统声称林肯纪念堂倒影池遭人破坏，并称已逮捕多人。然而，倒影池此前因修复工程失败而出现藻类泛滥、衬里破裂等问题，修复费用从150万升至1480万美元。批评者认为特朗普将公共问题政治化，动用执法力量应对公关危机。
topics.keywords: Reflecting Pool, vandalism, Trump, Jeanine Pirro, algae, rehabilitation project, no-bid contract

topics.title: 体育：世界杯亮点
topics.content: 2026年世界杯在美国举行，小国佛得角表现出色，战平乌拉圭，有望晋级淘汰赛。美国男足表现强劲，已锁定小组头名。赛事促进了文化交流，外国球迷在美国受到欢迎，但伊朗队遭遇美方不公待遇。
topics.keywords: World Cup, Cape Verde, USA, Iran, culture clash, knockout stage, Pochettino

topics.title: 英国政治：斯塔默辞职与工党领导权之争
topics.content: 英国首相基尔·斯塔默在任职近两年后辞职，原因是工党内部对其领导能力失去信心，以及一系列争议（如削减冬季燃料补贴、接受免费礼物、任命彼得·曼德尔森为驻美大使等）。工党将选举新领袖，曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆被视为热门人选。同时，改革党（Nigel Farage领导）在地方选举中表现强劲，对工党构成威胁。
topics.keywords: Keir Starmer, resignation, Labour Party, Andy Burnham, Reform UK, Nigel Farage, Brexit

topics.title: 美意关系：特朗普与意大利总理梅洛尼的公开争执
topics.content: 美国总统特朗普与意大利总理乔治娅·梅洛尼发生公开争执，起因是特朗普指责梅洛尼“乞求”与他合影，并称其支持率低。梅洛尼反驳称其言论“完全编造”。这场争执反映了特朗普与欧洲盟友之间的紧张关系，尤其是在美伊冲突和北约问题上。
topics.keywords: Donald Trump, Giorgia Meloni, feud, G7, NATO, U.S.-Iran conflict

topics.title: 美国政治：纽森拥抱拜登遗产的2028年总统竞选策略
topics.content: 加州州长加文·纽森据报道在2028年总统竞选中采取拥抱前总统乔·拜登遗产的策略，认为这能吸引黑人和拉丁裔选民。尽管拜登因退出连任竞选而声誉受损，纽森仍公开赞扬其政绩。这一策略与许多民主党人批评拜登形成对比，旨在在党内初选中占据独特位置。
topics.keywords: Gavin Newsom, Joe Biden, 2028 presidential election, Democratic Party, black voters, Latino voters

topics.title: 美伊核谈判与中东局势
topics.content: 副总统J.D.万斯在瑞士与伊朗进行高级别外交谈判，旨在达成长期和平协议。谈判涉及伊朗核计划、铀浓缩等问题，同时面临以色列与真主党冲突的干扰。美国未让以色列参与谈判，引发以色列不满。
topics.keywords: J.D. Vance, Iran, nuclear talks, Switzerland, Hezbollah, Israel, enriched uranium

topics.title: 美国国内：FISA监控工具续期争议
topics.content: 共和党参议员林赛·格雷厄姆警告特朗普总统不要让关键的对外监控工具（FISA 702条款）失效。特朗普因民主党可能让该条款失效而推迟了国家情报总监人选杰伊·克莱顿的确认。格雷厄姆认为失效将危及国家安全。
topics.keywords: FISA, Section 702, Lindsey Graham, Jay Clayton, DNI, surveillance
